overview: Processes Adobe FrameMaker MIF (Maker Interchange Format) documents. MIF is generated and read by Adobe FrameMaker; the filter supports MIF v8.0 and above. Encoding is automatically detected based on the file version, with MIF v8+ using UTF-8.

extraction.extractBodyPages.help: Controls whether text from the main document body pages is extracted for translation.

extraction.extractMasterPages.help: Controls whether text from master pages is extracted. Master pages define repeating page layouts (headers, footers, background text).

extraction.extractReferencePages.help: Controls whether text from reference pages is extracted. Note that FrameMaker creates new documents with several reference pages that already contain text by default, so disabling this avoids extracting that boilerplate content.
notes: By default FrameMaker creates new documents with several reference pages that contain text — review whether this content actually needs translation.

extraction.extractHiddenPages.help: Controls whether text from hidden pages is extracted for translation.

extraction.extractVariables.help: Extracts the **definitions** of FrameMaker variables. Variable definitions are the source text that gets expanded wherever the variable is used in the document.

extraction.extractIndexMarkers.help: Extracts index markers from the extractable pages. Each index entry's text is extracted as a **separate text unit**, placed before the text unit containing the index marker itself.

extraction.extractLinks.help: Extracts URLs from hypertext links in the extractable pages. Each URL is extracted as a **separate text unit**, placed before the text unit containing the hypertext marker.

extraction.extractHardReturnsAsText.help: Treats hard returns (manual line breaks) in MIF paragraphs as translatable text rather than structural formatting.

extraction.extractPgfNumFormatsInline.help: Extracts paragraph autonumber formats as inline content within the paragraph text.

extraction.extractReferenceFormats.help: Extracts cross-reference format definitions for translation.

inlineCodes.help:
Define regular expressions to match inline codes within extracted text. Any match is converted to an inline code (placeholder) in the extracted segments.

The default pattern for MIF is:
```
<\$.*?>
```
This matches FrameMaker variable references like `<$dayname>`, `<$monthnum>`, `<$year>` that appear inline in text.
examples: `<\$.*?>` — matches FrameMaker variable references such as `<$dayname>`, `<$fullfilename>`

limitations: Very large embedded insets (e.g. images) may cause Java heap memory issues. The workaround is to link to external objects rather than embed them.
The filter does not perform font mapping — if the target language requires different fonts, you must manually update the paragraph and character catalogs to use fonts with proper glyph support.
processingNotes: Input encoding is **automatically detected** based on the MIF file version and document metadata.
MIF v8 and above are always read and written as **UTF-8**.
